description: A reproducible probabilistic case study using the last four synthetic history weeks, a fixed Monte Carlo seed, and a handling-time improvement scenario that changes the staffing recommendation.

sensitivity.description: Reduce every category handling time by 20 percent to represent a faster booking workflow.

narrative.text: Schedule 9 reservation agents for the upcoming week. This recommendation provides an estimated 38.1% capacity confidence, with expected overtime of 9.3 hours, expected abandonment of 2.5 reservations, and an expected total economic cost of $8736.44. Compared with previous-week staffing, the recommended change is 0 agents, and compared with the manager plan, the change is +1 agents. Compared with the Balanced plan at 10 agents, this recommendation changes expected economic cost to $8736.44 while balancing capacity confidence and abandonment risk.
warnings: Expected abandonment remains above zero, so the recommendation still reflects some service-risk tradeoff.

narrative.text: Schedule 8 reservation agents for the upcoming week. This recommendation provides an estimated 94.0% capacity confidence, with expected overtime of 0.3 hours, expected abandonment of 0.1 reservations, and an expected total economic cost of $7071.58. Compared with previous-week staffing, the recommended change is -1 agents, and compared with the manager plan, the change is 0 agents. Compared with the Conservative plan at 9 agents, this recommendation changes expected economic cost to $7071.58 while balancing capacity confidence and abandonment risk.
warnings: Multiple named plans map to the same staffing level under the current confidence targets.
Expected abandonment remains above zero, so the recommendation still reflects some service-risk tradeoff.

executive_summary: Baseline recommendation: 9 agents. Sensitivity recommendation: 8 agents after applying 0.80x handling times.
